headline: Subsidy Rationalisation Savings Cited at ~RM15.5B by Budget 2026; Delivery Audit Still Limited
context: By Budget 2026, PM Anwar cited overall subsidy-rationalisation savings of about RM15.5 billion annually. PADU had compiled about 30.4 million individual profiles by June 2025 as one of the key reference databases for targeted RON95 distribution.

cards.big: Anwar cited ~RM15.5 billion annual savings from subsidy rationalisation in Budget 2026. Independent audit of targeting accuracy remains limited.
cards.sub: Diesel rationalisation (June 2024) and electricity restructuring contributed the bulk of savings to date. RON95 targeting begins in the second half of 2025.

cards.big: PADU compiled about 30.4 million individual profiles by June 2025. It serves as one of the key reference databases for MoF targeted-subsidy validation.
cards.sub: Public reporting has flagged data-quality concerns since launch. Rafizi Ramli resigned in 2025 after losing the PKR deputy-presidency race.

cards.big: By Budget 2026, electricity-rationalisation savings averaged ~RM6 billion/year. Diesel rationalisation contributed substantially in 2024-25.
cards.sub: Components have shifted as RON95 targeting begins. Read headline savings against verified component figures.

cards.big: Subsidy effectiveness reporting is still done largely by implementing ministries. Independent per-cohort audits remain limited.
cards.sub: Parliamentary questions have raised independent-audit calls. No separate independent auditor publishes per-cohort delivery results in available reporting.

cards.big: The savings number is now larger and better documented. Targeting-accuracy measurement by an independent party is the next test.
Like fixing a leaky pipe and announcing the savings before anyone independent has measured the new flow rate.
cards.sub: Headline savings are useful but incomplete evidence of policy success until delivery accuracy is independently audited.

cards.big: A reform proposal: stand up an independent audit publishing per-cohort delivery accuracy, separate from the implementing ministries' self-reporting.
